elements.title: manicule
elements.contents: ... that a manicule (pictured) in a manuscript of Cicero's Paradoxa Stoicorum had its fingers stretched to cover the full length of a passage?

elements.title: Ann Dallas
elements.contents: ... that by the time she met Queen Elizabeth II, Ann Dallas was amongst the last surviving members of the Kirkcudbright Artists' Colony?

elements.title: Choreographic Symphony "Maria Magdalena"
elements.contents: ... that Yamada's Choreographic Symphony "Maria Magdalena" is based on a ballet that couldn't be set due to religious concerns?

elements.title: Bunny Vosters
elements.contents: ... that Bunny Vosters and her daughter Gretchen Vosters Spruance had a winning streak that lasted over 15 years?

elements.title: Sancha of Aragon
elements.contents: ... that Sancha of Aragon took custody of her nephew Rodrigo after her release from papal imprisonment by agreement with his mother, Lucrezia Borgia?

elements.title: Stardust
elements.contents: ... that Danny Brown titled his album Stardust after contemplating on his failure to collaborate with the musician Sophie?

elements.title: I Witness Silwan
elements.contents: ... that the artworks of I Witness Silwan cover more than 610 metres (2,000 ft) of walling?

elements.title: Violet Chipao
elements.contents: ... that judge Violet Chipao's cases involving Malawian government ministers were stopped by the Director of Public Prosecutions?

elements.title: photo taken by Jeffrey Epstein
elements.contents: ... that a British newspaper paid $140,000 for a photograph of a photo taken by Jeffrey Epstein?
